title: catalpa × hybrida var. japonica
intro: Catalpa × hybrida var. japonica, also known as Japanese catalpa or Japanese bean tree, is a deciduous tree hybrid species derived from the cross between the Catalpa bignonioides and Catalpa ovata. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as sore throats, wounds, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Catalpa × hybrida var. japonica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused in oil to be used as a topical treatment for its medicinal properties.
